# UC-22.21.20 · Risk Management System — Hazard Log Update SLA (Art. 9/Annex IV)

## Description

Measures time from new_hazard to hazard_reviewed in risk register stream for RMS operational effectiveness.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_risk and sourcetypes rms:hazard, rms:hazard`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure hazard_id stable; (2) escalate unreviewed >7d; (3) link to incident index; (4) quarterly RMS committee export. Indexes required: `index=ai_risk`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=rms:hazard`, `sourcetype=rms:hazard``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_risk` `sourcetype=rms:hazard` — hazard_id, status, transition.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure hazard_id stable; (2) escalate unreviewed >7d; (3) link to incident index; (4) quarterly RMS committee export.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_risk sourcetype="rms:hazard" earliest=-90d
| eval opened=if(status="new",_time,null())
| eval reviewed=if(status="reviewed",_time,null())
| stats min(opened) as t_open, max(reviewed) as t_rev by hazard_id
| eval review_hours=round((t_rev-t_open)/3600,2)
| where isnull(t_rev) OR review_hours>168
| sort - review_hours
```

Understanding this SPL

**Risk Management System — Hazard Log Update SLA (Art. 9/Annex IV) (EU AI Act)** — Measures time from new_hazard to hazard_reviewed in risk register stream for RMS operational effectiveness.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_risk` `sourcetype=rms:hazard` — hazard_id, status, transition. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_risk; **sourcetype**: rms:hazard. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_risk, sourcetype="rms:hazard", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opened** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **reviewed** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by hazard_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **review_hours** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(t_rev) OR review_hours>168` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Histogram (review_hours), Table (open hazards), Single value (mean review_hours)

## SPL

```spl
index=ai_risk sourcetype="rms:hazard" earliest=-90d
| eval opened=if(status="new",_time,null())
| eval reviewed=if(status="reviewed",_time,null())
| stats min(opened) as t_open, max(reviewed) as t_rev by hazard_id
| eval review_hours=round((t_rev-t_open)/3600,2)
| where isnull(t_rev) OR review_hours>168
| sort - review_hours
```

## Visualization

Histogram (review_hours), Table (open hazards), Single value (mean review_hours)
